headline: NVIDIA’s Sovereign AI Deals And Meta Alliance Shape Valuation Debate
summary: NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) has agreed a multiyear partnership with Meta to deploy millions of Blackwell and Rubin GPUs and expand confidential computing for privacy focused AI. In India, the company is supporting one of Asia’s largest AI superclusters with Yotta Data Services and backing a sovereign AI venture under India's AI Mission. NVIDIA hardware powers India's first "Make in India" supercomputer and new sovereign and secure AI factories in Australia and Canada. Additional alliances include...

headline: Qualcomm and Other Industry Leaders Commit to 6G Trajectory Towards Commercialization Starting from 2029 Onwards
summary: BARCELONA, Spain, March 02, 2026--Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced a new strategic coalition with leading industry partners to accelerate the development and global deployment of 6G. Revealed at MWC Barcelona 2026, the collaboration establishes a clear, milestone-driven roadmap focused on delivering 6G commercial systems starting from 2029 onwards.

content.title: Qualcomm and Other Industry Leaders Commit to 6G Trajectory Towards Commercialization Starting from 2029 Onwards
content.summary: BARCELONA, Spain, March 02, 2026--Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced a new strategic coalition with leading industry partners to accelerate the development and global deployment of 6G. Revealed at MWC Barcelona 2026, the collaboration establishes a clear, milestone-driven roadmap focused on delivering 6G commercial systems starting from 2029 onwards.

headline: AMD’s Meta And Nutanix Deals Reframe AI Growth And Shareholder Trade Offs
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NasdaqGS:AMD) has entered multi year agreements with Meta and Nutanix that aim to reshape hyperscale and enterprise AI infrastructure. The Meta deal includes up to 6 gigawatts of custom AMD GPUs and CPUs, plus performance based equity warrants that could give Meta up to 10% ownership in AMD. With Nutanix, AMD plans to build and commercialize an open, agentic AI platform targeted at enterprise and cloud customers. At a share price of $200.21, Advanced Micro Devices...

headline: AI Matters More Than Iran for Stocks, Says Citi
summary: Artificial intelligence poses more of a threat to stocks than the Iran war right now, according to Citi. "As of this writing, we are probably more concerned about longer-term AI-influenced deflation than military conflict influence on oil price and inflation," analyst Scott T.

headline: AES to Be Taken Private in $33 Billion Deal. Why the Stock Is Falling Sharply.
summary: AES  the utilities and clean energy company, is being taken private by an investor consortium that includes an  EQT Group  infrastructure fund and  BlackRock  -owned Global Infrastructure Partners.  The consortium, which also includes the Qatar Investment Authority and the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, will pay $15 a share for the world’s largest supplier of clean energy to corporations.  AES stock fell 17% to $14.40 in premarket trading Monday.

content.title: AES to Be Taken Private in $33 Billion Deal. Why the Stock Is Falling Sharply.

The consortium, which also includes the Qatar Investment Authority and the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, will pay $15 a share for the world’s largest supplier of clean energy to corporations.  AES stock fell 17% to $14.40 in premarket trading Monday.

headline: Meta Resets AI Hardware Plan With AMD And Google Partnerships
summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has halted its in-house Olympus AI accelerator project after running into technical and manufacturing hurdles. The company has signed multi billion dollar agreements with AMD and Google to secure large volumes of AI chips and cloud compute capacity. Meta is considering taking a stake of up to 10% in AMD as part of a broader shift toward a diversified AI hardware supply chain. These moves are set to influence how Meta builds and delivers AI powered products...

content.title: Meta Resets AI Hardware Plan With AMD And Google Partnerships
content.summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has halted its in-house Olympus AI accelerator project after running into technical and manufacturing hurdles. The company has signed multi billion dollar agreements with AMD and Google to secure large volumes of AI chips and cloud compute capacity. Meta is considering taking a stake of up to 10% in AMD as part of a broader shift toward a diversified AI hardware supply chain. These moves are set to influence how Meta builds and delivers AI powered products...
